subject: Beyond Basic Translation: Building Multilingual AI Apps That Actually Work
body:
Hello First Name,

This week's 🦄 ai that works session was all about "Beyond Basic Translation: Building Multilingual AI Apps That Actually Work"!

The full recording, code, and diagrams from the session are now available on GitHub:
https://github.com/hellovai/ai-that-works

We covered a lot on designing flexible, cross-lingual prompt architectures for AI applications. Here's a quick rundown:

**Avoid Direct Translation Fails**: Simply translating user input and expecting a complex, English-steered AI pipeline to respond correctly in another language often leads to inconsistent performance and English-biased outputs.
**The Robust Pipeline Approach**: Instead of building parallel language pipelines or relying on the LLM's instruction following, build a core, well-tested English pipeline. Pre-process user input (translate, capture intent) into English, run your agent, then translate the English response back to the user's original language and tone.
**Benefits & Optimizations**: This layered approach significantly improves accuracy, maintainability, and evaluation. You can also add heuristics to detect primary language users (e.g., English) and bypass translation for lower latency, ensuring a high-quality experience for all users.

If there's one key takeaway from this session, it's this: For reliable multilingual AI applications, think of language translation as a pre- and post-processing layer around a single, well-engineered core business logic pipeline. Don't rely on the LLM to handle complex multilingual steering directly.

Last week, we also had a session on "Advanced Prompt Engineering Techniques" for handling complex language tasks. You can find the video, whiteboards, and code on GitHub too!

Tomorrow's session is "Building Truly Global AI Products." We'll explore designing AI apps that seamlessly serve users worldwide. Sign up here: https://lu.ma/zcf5c8yd

If you have any questions, just reply to this email or ask on Discord: https://www.boundaryml.com/discord. We read every message! Happy coding 🧑‍💻

Vaibhav & Dex
call_to_action: Sign up for our next session on "Building Truly Global AI Products"
